tweets.text:
💭Everyone’s self-deportation process is different. You may want to let your employer, your friends and your family know you’re leaving. You may also want to pack belongings or make living arrangements for the next phase of your journey.

Remember, if ICE officials arrest you, you may not have an opportunity to do any of these things. 

Learn your options on ICE’s self-deportation webpage⬇️

tweets.text:
ICE encountered Dipakkumar Ghelani, an Indian national illegally present in the United States, at the Duval County Pre-Trial Detention Facility April 16. 

🔎One of ICE’s 287(g) law enforcement partners, the Florida Highway Patrol, arrested Ghelani on a felony warrant for trafficking drugs with an estimated street value of over $13 million.

📰Learn more in our newsroom.

tweets.text:
Ruowei Liu, a criminal illegal alien from China, was convicted of prostitution and keeping a bawdy house in Virginia. 

She came to the U.S. legally on a vistor visa via the Detroit Metropolitan Airport port of entry last year, but she let her visa expire. 

ICE took custody of her upon her release from the Virginia Beach City Jail.

tweets.text:
Police in Brown County, Wisc., arrested David Joel Mairena-Garcia, of Nicaragua, after he allegedly used a key to enter his ex-girlfriend's home, beat her and hacked a man with a machete April 20.

The male victim was wounded on his shoulder, forearm and shin. The ex-girlfriend  suffered lacerations on her hand and neck, plus she had a head injury. 

Police in Green Bay later arrested him on two counts of attempted murder, two counts of aggravated assault and armed burglary.

ICE @EROChicago lodged a detainer with the Brown County Jail asking officials not to release him back into the community, but to hold him for ICE instead after he’s faced justice.

tweets.text:
Atharva Vyas, a 24-year-old Indian illegal alien, allegedly violently assaulted a mother named Gabriella Perez and her three-year-old daughter in a San Antonio park April 18.

Vyas allegedly even bit the toddler and knocked out two of her teeth.

ICE lodged a detainer with local law enforcement asking them to turn Vyas over to ICE instead of releasing him into the community after he’s faced justice for his alleged crimes.

tweets.text:
ICE @HSINewark successfully located and recovered 15 stolen Ceres Air C31 drones equipped with advanced chemical spraying capabilities, during an April 27 targeted operation. 

The drones were discovered during a separate and unrelated investigation into cargo theft at a warehouse in Dover, NJ., in coordination with the @NJSP.

No additional information is available at this time as this is an ongoing investigation.

tweets.text:
ICE @EROChicago issued a detainer for Yefry Archaga-Elvir, an illegal alien from Honduras who reportedly targeted, lured, and murdered 15-year-old Miles Young in Missouri. 

Prosecutors allege Archaga lured the 15-year-old boy to his death by tricking him into thinking he was meeting a girl. Instead, he was ambushed and begged for his own life, prosecutors say. After the murder, Archaga allegedly made a phone call and bragged about his actions.

ICE HSI and the U.S. Marshals Service arrested Yechiel Yehoshua Farkash, a foreign fugitive from Israel who was wanted in his home nation for alleged sex crimes against children. Our team arrested him at his home in Lakewood, N.J.

ICE HSI Atlantic City will coordinate with ICE @HSINewark Victim Assistance Program to conduct any additional interviews and identify other possible victims related to the case.

author.description: Protects America through criminal investigations and by enforcing immigration law to preserve national security and public safety.  https://t.co/rDI2IeFofc

tweets.text:
James Andrew Wellesley, of the UK, will serve a 10-year prison sentence and must pay $1M in forfeiture, following a $100M Ponzi scheme involving a fake wine company. He was sentenced April 20.

Wellesley had two prior convictions in the U.K., both related to financial fraud. He was extradited to the U.S. last year to face justice for his investment fraud scheme.

Wellesley and his partner posed as executives at Bordeaux Cellars and raised nearly $100 million by promising investors regular interest payments from high net worth wine collectors. This scheme targeted nearly 140 individuals located around the world, including 71 people in the U.S., 21 in the U.K., and 10 in Hong Kong.

ICE HSI was proud to be a part of the FBI-led team that investigated this case.

tweets.text:
Minh Duy Tong Nguyen aka Tony Playboy, of Vietnam, was once a fugitive wanted for the murder of two people and the attempted murder of three others. He was featured on an episode of "America’s Most Wanted" in 1990 and subsequently captured in Canada and extradited to the U.S. to stand trial for murder. 

He was sentenced to 45-years in the state prison.

ICE @EROHouston took custody of him April 16, upon his release from prison.

His deportation order is over 25 years old.

He’s about to go home.

tweets.text:
ICE Homeland Security Investigations Newark special agents, as part of the Homeland Security Task Force and in close partnership with our federal, state and local counterparts, announced a significant achievement April 23.

Our team made huge strides in combatting drug trafficking in New Jersey, as part of an ongoing investigation, with the seizure of:

➡️15 kilos of fentanyl
➡️3 kilos of cocaine
➡️19 firearms
➡️A 3D printer with ghost gun manufacturing capabilities

“Today’s operation marks a decisive step forward in our ongoing efforts to stem the flow of illicit opioids, secure our borders, and confront the fentanyl crisis that endangers lives across our state,” said ICE @HSINewark Special Agent in Charge Michael S. McCarthy. “New Jersey is safer as a result of these actions, and HSI Newark will continue to pursue those who threaten our communities with relentless determination.”

tweets.text:
Haydee Mercado-Rodriguez is an MS-13 gang member from El Salvador sentenced to 228 years for:
⚖️AGGRAVATED HOMICIDE 2X
⚖️AGGRAVATED EXTORTION
⚖️PARTICIPATION IN A TERROIST ORGANIZATION

She fled to the U.S. to evade justice for her crimes, where the Biden administration’s open-door policy welcomed her with open arms — endangering the Texas community in which she lived.

ICE @EROHouston arrested this foreign fugitive April 9, and she’ll remain in ICE custody until her removal.

tweets.text:
ICE @HSI_DC special agents arrested Roni Mendez-Escobar during a targeted enforcement operation, April 22, on federal criminal charges of possessing and distributing CHILD SEX ABUSE MATERIAL. 

Mendez-Escobar is a previously deported criminal illegal from Guatemala who was charged with 15 counts of possessing CSAM in Fairfax County, Va., last year.

At that time, ICE lodged a detainer against him with Fairfax County, asking the local authorities to turn him over to ICE instead of releasing him back into the community.

Fairfax County refused our immigration detainer, released him and dismissed the charges last October.

He’s currently in U.S. Marshals’ custody awaiting prosecution on the new CSAM charges.

—Assault and battery with a dangerous weapon
—Assault and battery on a correctional institution guard

ICE arrested him in Massachusetts upon his release from the Worcester County Sheriff’s Office.

He has a 2024 final removal order on file and will be deported.

tweets.text:
ICE @EROBoston removed Jairon Gutierrez-Hernandez, a criminal illegal alien from Guatemala with a 2021 final removal order and pending criminal charges for arson and property violations.

According to police in Waltham, Mass., Gutierrez-Hernandez allegedly admitted in Spanish to a translating officer that he was the person who set a vehicle on fire.

Despite his status as an illegal alien, a judge released Gutierrez-Hernandez on personal recognizance back into the community where he was arrested by federal agents on immigration violations.

tweets.text:
Hilda Esperanza Lopez-Hernandez fled the scene of a deadly accident while driving a red Ford Mustang in Fort Walton Beach, Fla., in 2020. Then, she called her boyfriend to switch vehicles with her.

The fatal hit-and-run incident should never have happened. She had no license, and she wasn’t supposed to be in this country. 

Lopez-Hernandez was convicted in Okaloosa County of hit-and-run, operating a motor vehicle without a license and hiding evidence Jan. 9, 2023. She was handed a four-year sentence.  

ICE Miami arrested her after she was released from jail and deported her April 12, 2026.

tweets.text:
A Fairfax County judge sentenced 18-year-old criminal illegal alien Israel Christopher Flores Ortiz, of El Salvador, to jail for groping several female classmates in the hallways of his Virginia high school earlier this year.

ICE would like to take custody of him for immigration law violations upon his release, so ICE @EROWashington officers placed a detainer with the Fairfax County Adult Detention Center March 13

The problem? Virginia is a sanctuary jurisdiction and sanctuary jurisdictions, by definition, don’t honor ICE detainers.

That means that when he’s released, ICE must assemble a team and find him in the community.

tweets.text:
When local authorities honor detainers, aliens can’t return to the community to reoffend and fewer ICE officers are needed.

Take Daniel Jesus Piedra-Fernandez, of Colombia, as an example:

👉He’s a violent aggravated felon with convictions for robbery, battery, burglary, property damage and domestic violence. 
👉He has a final order of removal and a recent arrest. 
👉ICE Salt Lake City took custody of him within the security of a local jail because officials there honored our detainer.

In a sanctuary jurisdiction, Piedra-Fernandez would have been released back into the community, and ICE would have to assemble a large team to apprehend him at-large.
tweets.text_resolved:

tweets.text:
SANCTUARIES HELP CUBAN ILLEGAL ALIEN AUTHOR DECADES-LONG RAP SHEET

Despite a 1980 final removal order, lenient sentences from NY and MD enabled repeat offender Eledoro Valenzuela Rodriguez to amass these convictions, along with numerous charges, like battery, being dismissed: 
 
1984 | NY County, NY: Weapon possession – 30 days 
1985 | Prince George’s County, MD: controlled substance possession – 19 days (suspended) 
1986 | NY County, NY: Possession of a controlled substance – three years’ probation 
1989 | Marion County, IN: Cocaine possession and dealing – 20 years and 5 years (concurrent) 
2025 | Miami, FL: Cocaine possession with intent to sell, felon possessing a firearm and ammunition, and trespassing after warning – pending. 
 
Clearly unreformed, ICE Miami officers took him into custody from a county jail in Florida and will ensure he’s deported quickly.

tweets.text:
Arlington County police arrested Luzvin Garcia Moran, of Guatemala, for allegedly trying to sexually assault a Virginia woman. 

Garcia Moran is facing charges of abduction of a person with intent to defile, sodomy by force or victim helplessness, and assault.

➡️ICE lodged an immigration detainer with the Arlington County jail in Virginia asking them not to release him back into the community —Unfortunately, Gov. Spanberger doesn’t want to work with ICE.

🔎 ICE will arrest him for violating federal immigration law once he’s released. Our officers could pick him up safely at the jail if the detainer is honored, but thanks to the state’s sanctuary policies, we may have to assemble a larger team to find him in the community.

tweets.text:
ICE Fugitive Operations arrested Altieris Chaves Paiva during a targeted operation April 5 near Falmouth, Mass., thanks to a tip.

Chaves Paiva, a criminal illegal alien from Brazil, is wanted in Brazil for HOMICIDE, but he has a criminal record in Massachusetts, too.

Police in Oak Bluffs arrested him in 2024 for drunk driving, negligent driving and another traffic offense, but he pleaded guilty to only negligent driving. He was given probation and continued to live freely in the community.

This fugitive will remain in ICE custody pending removal proceedings.

tweets.text:
Today we honor the legacy of Senior Special Agents Paul D. Ice and Claude A. Medearis who were killed in the 1995 Oklahoma City Alfred P. Murrah Federal Building bombing. 

🎗️ICE remembers you and pledges its continued resolve to ensure public safety and national security. 

💻See their stories: https://t.co/nDoP5OAtA1

author.description: Protects America through criminal investigations and by enforcing immigration law to preserve national security and public safety.  https://t.co/rDI2IeFofc

tweets.text:
If you or someone you care about has witnessed or been affected by a crime linked to immigration, ICE’s VOICE Office is here to support those victims and their families. 

📞 855-48-VOICE (6423) https://t.co/OJBzeEmDLe

tweets.text:
Today we honor the legacy of Special Agent Michael J. Reimers who was killed during a Los Angeles drug raid in 1979. 

🎗️ICE remembers you and pledges its continued resolve to ensure public safety and national security. 

💻See their stories: https://t.co/vHCZX9rglF https://t.co/4QbUTRkYZb
